text: The surgeon retracts the bladder upwards at the twelve o'clock position while manipulating the vagina using a prograsper. Two needle drivers are visible in the surgical field, facilitating precise tissue handling. The video speed is increased to show the procedure more efficiently.

The surgeon uses a V-lock suture and a 3-0 absorbable suture to perform placation of the pulvocervical fascia. The procedure begins distally and progresses proximally, with the prograsper used to adjust tension. The placation is performed from left to right, with seven to twelve sutures placed depending on the patient's anatomy.
The surgeon places mesh on the anterior and posterior aspects of the surgical site, ensuring proper coverage for hernia repair.
